content: Antibiotic resistance is a growing threat that affects us all, from the health of our workforce to the stability of our economy. In simple terms, it occurs when bacteria evolve to withstand the drugs designed to kill them. This happens largely due to the overuse and misuse of antibiotics in both human medicine and agriculture. When we use antibiotics too much or for the wrong reasons, we inadvertently help bacteria become stronger and more resistant. The consequences are dire: more severe illnesses, longer hospital stays, and increased healthcare costs. For businesses, this means a less productive workforce and higher insurance costs. The economic burden on societies is also significant. It's crucial for all of us, including business leaders, to understand and address this issue by promoting responsible antibiotic use and supporting initiatives to combat resistance. Our collective health and economic well-being depend on it.
